seoTitle: How to Accept PayPal Pending Money Fast (2026)
metaDescription: Find your unclaimed PayPal payments and manually approve them before they automatically return to the sender after 30 days.
intro: If money someone sent you hasn't shown up in your available balance, it may be sitting as pending or unclaimed until you take action. This guide walks through locating that payment and manually accepting it. It's for anyone who's noticed incoming funds stuck in a waiting state.

steps.heading: Go to your Activity feed
steps.body: Log in to PayPal and click Activity to view your recent transaction rows.

steps.heading: Find the pending transaction
steps.body: Look for the incoming payment tagged Pending or Unclaimed.

steps.heading: Open the transaction row
steps.body: Click into the transaction to expand its details and locate the action buttons.

steps.heading: Click Accept or Approve
steps.body: Select the button to begin manually claiming the funds.

steps.heading: Confirm currency and submit
steps.body: Choose whether to open a balance in the sent currency or decline, then click Submit to instantly claim your funds.

faq.question: Why do some payments require manual acceptance?
faq.answer: Payments often need manual approval when your account is missing a confirmed email, phone number, or matching currency balance.

faq.question: What happens if I never accept the payment?
faq.answer: Unclaimed transfers are typically returned automatically to the sender after about 30 days.

faq.question: Does accepting a payment cost me anything?
faq.answer: Accepting itself is free, though currency conversion fees may apply if the payment was sent in a different currency.

faq.question: Can I decline a payment instead of accepting it?
faq.answer: Yes, the same screen that lets you accept also gives you the option to deny the transfer.
